title: Understanding Angular Momentum
text: Angular momentum (L) for a rolling object is given by the formula $$L=I \omega$$, where I is the moment of inertia and ω is the angular velocity.

title: Moment of Inertia for a Sphere
text: The moment of inertia (I) for a uniform solid sphere is $$I=\frac{2}{5} m r^2$$. Use this to calculate I for the boulder.

title: Relating Linear and Angular Velocity
text: The angular velocity (ω) can be found using the relation $$v=r \omega$$, where v is the linear velocity and $$r$$ is the radius of the sphere.

title: Finding Linear Velocity
text: Use energy conservation to find the linear velocity halfway down the hill. The potential energy at the top is converted into kinetic energy (both translational and rotational) halfway down.

title: Calculate Moment of Inertia
text: Calculate the moment of inertia (I) for the boulder using $$I=\frac{2}{5} m r^2$$, where $$m=20$$ kg and $$r=0.2$$ $$m$$.

title: Calculate Linear Velocity
text: Use energy conservation: $$m g h=\frac{1}{2} m v^2+\frac{1}{2} I {\omega}^2$$. Solve for v halfway down the hill.

title: Calculate Angular Velocity
text: Use the relation $$v=r \omega$$ to find ω, where $$v=8.94$$ $$\frac{m}{s}$$ and $$r=0.2$$ $$m$$.

title: Calculate Angular Momentum
text: Finally, calculate the angular momentum $$L=I \omega$$ using $$I=1.6$$ $$kg m^2$$ and $$ω=44.7$$ $$\frac{rad}{s}$$.
